step.title: Template Selection
step.goal: Select the per-agent files that will compose this agent's source. Shared structural blocks (activation, agent-activation, baseline, agent-close) are auto-selected based on mode. Per-agent files are filled during creation.

substep.title: Auto-Select Shared Includes
substep.action:
Based on mode from Step 1, determine which SHARED files from .vera/shared/ this agent will include via agent.yaml. The shared blocks live in one place and are referenced by relative path — not copied INTO the agent directory.
Primary agent (canonical 8-include order): 1. frontmatter.md (per-agent) 2. shared/activation.md (preamble + outer fence) 3. shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md (optional — only if agent reads CONTEXT.md) 4. shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md (optional — only if agent uses thoughts CLI) 5. shared/agent-activation.md (open tag + complete activation block) 6. persona.md (per-agent) 7. menu.md (per-agent) 8. shared/agent-close.md (close tag + close fence)
Subagent (canonical 7-include order): 1. frontmatter.md (per-agent) 2. shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md (optional — only if agent reads CONTEXT.md) 3. shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md (optional — only if agent uses thoughts CLI) 4. shared/subagent-open.md (<subagent id="..."> open tag + ```xml fence) 5. persona.md (per-agent — 4-tag <persona> block) 6. instructions.md (per-agent — <input-expectations> + <workflow>) 7. shared/subagent-close.md (</subagent> + ``` close fence)
Shared blocks use {{variables}} from agent.yaml for agent-specific substitution (agent_id, character_name, agent_title, emoji). The 4-layer merge supplies {{user_name}} and {{communication_language}} from the user's ~/.config/vera/config.yaml.

substep.title: Detect Missing Project Infrastructure
substep.action:
Before finalizing the includes list, verify that each referenced shared/ file EXISTS in the current project. agent-creator bundles canonical copies of these files at: references/baselines/thoughts-cli-awareness.md references/baselines/context-md-awareness.md references/shared-fragments/activation.md references/shared-fragments/agent-activation.md references/shared-fragments/agent-close.md references/shared-fragments/subagent-open.md references/shared-fragments/subagent-close.md
For each include this agent will use, check if the corresponding project file exists. If missing, the actual copy operation happens in Step 3 (Scaffold) — for now, record which bundled references will need to be materialized into the project's shared/ directory.
Use bash list operations to check existence: ls .vera/shared/activation.md ls .vera/shared/agent-activation.md ls .vera/shared/agent-close.md ls .vera/shared/subagent-open.md ls .vera/shared/subagent-close.md ls .vera/shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md ls .vera/shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md
Build a {{missing_infrastructure}} list of files that need to be copied from bundle to project. This list drives the copy operation in Step 3.

substep.title: Identify Per-Agent Files Needed
substep.action:
Based on mode, determine which per-agent files this agent needs:
Primary agent (always needs): - frontmatter.md — YAML frontmatter (mode, color, description, permissions) - persona.md — <persona> block (role, identity, communication_style, principles) - menu.md — <menu> block (skill items + MH/CH built-ins)
Subagent (always needs): - frontmatter.md — YAML frontmatter (mode: subagent or all, permission map, description) - persona.md — <persona> block (SAME 4-tag shape as primary; capability boundaries live in <principles>) - instructions.md — <input-expectations> + <workflow> ONLY (no <scope> tag; no <agent> wrapper)
Optional baseline includes (BOTH primary and subagent): - shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md — if agent reads project glossaries - shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md — if agent uses thoughts CLI
Position of baseline includes: - Primary: between shared/activation.md and shared/agent-activation.md - Subagent: between frontmatter.md and persona.md Rationale: baselines establish environmental context before the agent's identity activates.

substep.title: Confirm Include Composition
substep.template-output:
Present the complete agent.yaml `includes:` list in build order:
Primary agent example: ```yaml includes: - frontmatter.md - shared/activation.md - shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/agent-activation.md - persona.md - menu.md - shared/agent-close.md ```
Subagent example: ```yaml includes: - frontmatter.md - shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md # if applicable - persona.md - instructions.md ```
This is the order vera-cli will concatenate at build time.
If {{missing_infrastructure}} from substep 2a-bis is non-empty, confirm with the user that those bundled references will be copied to the project's shared/ directory in Step 3.
[a] Adjust [c] Continue [y] YOLO

section.title: Include Order Matters
section.description: vera-cli concatenates includes in declared order. The order defines what the LLM sees first in its system prompt AND determines XML tag opening/closing pairings.

rules.rule: frontmatter.md MUST be first (provides the OpenCode YAML frontmatter at top of file)
shared/activation.md comes second (preamble + outer <agent-activation> fence — primary agents only)
shared/baseline/* baseline awareness fragments come BEFORE shared/agent-activation.md so the agent has baseline awareness before its identity activates
shared/agent-activation.md opens the <agent> tag and runs the activation block
persona.md and menu.md fill in identity inside the open <agent> tag
shared/agent-close.md MUST be last (closes the <agent> tag and the outer code fence)

section.title: When to Include Optional Baseline Fragments

rules.rule: context-md-awareness: include when the agent reads CONTEXT.md or CONTEXT-MAP.md for project vocabulary. Reader-side only — separate from the context-md-format skill which is for WRITING.
thoughts-cli-awareness: include when the agent calls `thoughts metadata`, `thoughts validate`, `thoughts backlog`, etc. Most workflow agents that produce thoughts-tracked artifacts need this.
Skip baseline fragments if they add no value — fewer includes means leaner system prompt.
